summary: Define Save Envelope v1, snapshot schema, round-trip load/save, and M1 golden fixtures
acceptance_criteria: Save Envelope v1 has explicit magic, schemaVersion, build/source metadata, contentManifestHash, scenario ID, seed, currentDay, checksum, authoritative snapshot DTO, scheduler data, and short command/event tail.
Permanent save format is an explicit DTO/codec with schema validation; directly JSON.stringify-ing hydrated WorldState object graphs is forbidden.

Load path validates envelope, checksum, schema version, size/depth limits, references, semantic invariants, and returns structured rejection reasons without mutating the current committed state on failure.
Minimal save/load round-trip over the M1 graph fixture preserves state hash and scheduler/RNG compatibility hooks.
Corrupt checksum, unknown version, malformed payload, bad ID reference, and contentManifestHash mismatch are safely rejected.
Golden save fixture and compatibility test plan exist; intentional schema/hash changes require a recorded reason and golden update.
Node runner and Worker request-save/load-save paths share the same core save codec; browser IndexedDB and Electron filesystem adapters are out of scope.
Same seed, initial state, command tail, and loaded snapshot produce the same hash in Node and Worker path.
M1 performance baseline records 3650-day headless run timing plus save size and save/load timing for the 30-node fixture; no premature optimization or new runtime database is introduced.
required_tests: Golden fixture tests cover save round-trip, hash preservation, and intentional fixture update workflow.
Malformed/corrupt save tests cover checksum, unknown schema, bad reference, truncated payload, and contentManifestHash mismatch.
Cross-environment contract tests cover Node runner and Worker save/load producing identical final hashes.
notes: Save schema is R3. If implementation changes compatibility strategy beyond docs/02 and ADR-004, write a new ADR before the codec implementation and route architecture questions to systems_architect.
Do not implement platform file IO, IndexedDB persistence, cloud saves, migrations beyond v1 scaffolding, compression, or full event sourcing in this task.
